weaknesses: No scenario or client investigation provided
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission contains no real-world context, no client consultation, and no measurable success criteria. All three strands fall into the zero band, so Planning is awarded 0.

strengths: Object-oriented class diagram demonstrating design structure
Flowcharts effectively communicate game logic and timer behavior
Use of arrays, classes, and GUI components shows some tool utilization
weaknesses: Algorithms are modest in complexity and largely standard
No code snippets or detailed pseudocode provided
No source documentation or attribution of external references
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Development exhibits a moderate level of complexity and appropriate use of standard tools, placing it around the mid-band of the 9â12 range. The absence of any documentation or source attribution holds it back from higher marks. Awarding 6/12 reflects its relative strength compared to other criteria while accounting for these gaps.

strengths: Comprehensive wireframes showing two interface versions
Multiple flowcharts covering core logic and navigation
A structured test plan outlining six functional tests
weaknesses: No chronological record of tasks or timeline
Test plan missing expected outcomes and pass/fail criteria
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The submission offers a clear design overview (wireframes, flowcharts, class diagram) and a basic but structured test plan, indicative of band 5â6 performance overall. Although the record of tasks is absent, the depth and coherence of the design and testing artifacts justify full marks (6/6) in Solution Overview.

weaknesses: No evidence of product functionality (no video or live demo)
No discussion of extensibility or future modifications
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: With no demonstration of a working product and no mention of potential expansion, both strands fall into the zero band. Functionality and Extensibility is therefore awarded 0.
Holistic AI evaluation: There is no reflective evaluation, no feedback from a client or adviser, and no realistic recommendations. All strands are in the zero band, so Evaluation receives 0.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student supplies several artefacts: wire-frame screenshots of two versions of the interface, multiple flowcharts (answer processing, timer, statistics, overall navigation), and a rudimentary class diagram for riddle storage. While explanations are minimal, these diagrams collectively give a basic, partially coherent picture of the proposed system. That aligns best with the âbasic design overview showing partial understandingâ descriptor (band 3-4); a mid-band score of 3 is appropriate.

No explanation is given as to why a riddle-solving game is an appropriate product for an identified client; therefore a product rationale is entirely absent and the strand must be placed in the zero band.

The student mentions use of arrays, classes and a timer but provides no evidence of advanced tool utilisation (e.g., external libraries, databases, deployment scripts). Some appropriate use of standard tools is implied, situating the work near the middle of the 1-4 band; 2 marks are therefore assigned.

A table outlines six functional tests covering interface navigation, difficulty selection, display of riddles, answer evaluation, and reset behaviour. Although expected results, test data, and pass/fail outcomes are missing, the plan is more than âlimitedâ and fits the definition of a âbasic outline test planâ. Band 3-4 therefore applies, with 3 marks awarded for its partial yet serviceable scope.
The student does not outline future improvements or recommendations, realistic or otherwise, so the strand must receive zero.
No bibliographic references or attributions are visible, nor is there any explanation of specific programming techniques. This lack of documentation places the strand in the zero band.
No chronological record of tasks (such as dated entries, descriptions of activities, problems, or next steps) is provided in the materials shown, so the strand falls in band 0.

The proposed productâan object-oriented riddle game with timer, scoring, statistics and three difficulty levelsâdemonstrates only modest algorithmic depth. Logic is straightforward and reuses standard GUI/time mechanisms; ingenuity is low but present. Band 1-4 is suitable, and 2 marks reflect the limited complexity.
The extract contains no formal, measurable success criteria; hence there is nothing that could be considered even âgenerally inappropriateâ criteria. The zero band is therefore appropriate.
No comments, quotations, or survey data from a client or adviser appear in the submission; the zero band applies.
The materials do not discuss how the product could be expanded (e.g., adding new riddle sets, database support, multiplayer). Therefore, no evidence exists to justify any mark for extensibility.

The submission extract contains no narrative describing a real-world scenario, no identification of an authentic client, and no evidence of any consultation. Consequently, none of the qualities required even for the lowest described band are met.
The submission excerpt does not include a demonstration video or written confirmation that the application executes. Without evidence of operation, the strand must be awarded zero.
There is no reflective section comparing final performance against success criteria (indeed none were defined). The evaluation strand is therefore in the zero band.

annotations.comment: Consider adding negative or boundary tests (e.g., invalid inputs, time-out behavior) to cover edge cases beyond the core functionality.
The empty table above lacks any record of tasks or project timeline. A chronological log of activities would demonstrate planning and development flow.
Version 1.0 wireframe provides an initial visual concept, showing effort in early design. This artifact supports understanding of the intended interface layout.
The diagrams are presented without explanatory annotations. Adding labels or brief notes to each component would clarify their roles in the design.
No context or client scenario is provided alongside the âRiddle Solverâ title. A clear project brief would ground the design in real user needs.
The label âAnsserâ contains a typo. Correcting spelling errors ensures professional presentation and avoids misinterpretation.
The form table outlining buttons (Show solution, Next Riddle, Change Difficulty) is a useful UI outline. Enhance by specifying expected button behaviors.
After-solution GUI mockup shows user feedback state, but lacks explanation of how scoring or timing influences this screen. Include notes on data binding.
Iteration to Version 2.0 shows commitment to refinement. The final wireframes detail multiple states, reflecting planned interface progression.
The final version screens lack a concise narrative describing the improvements made since Version 1.0. Include a brief summary of changes to demonstrate design evolution.
Flow diagram for inputting an answer clearly maps the process steps. Consider adding start/end symbols and decision nodes for clarity.
The class diagram for RiddleManager and Riddle objects demonstrates use of object-oriented design. Detailing method parameters and return types would strengthen this documentation.
The test plan includes a solid variety of functional tests but lacks explicit expected results and pass/fail criteria. Adding these will make the testing more measurable and actionable.
The description for the âReset Gameâ test is truncated and unclear. Ensure each stepâs nature and expected outcome are fully described to avoid ambiguity.
The âView statisticsâ flowchart is extensive but unlabeled. Annotating key transitions (e.g., data updates) would improve comprehension.
The data management description mentions an array of Riddle objects but omits how persistence or updates occur. Specify storage or retrieval mechanisms.
The timer algorithm description highlights scoring impact based on speed. Including pseudocode or flow steps would clarify its integration into the game loop.
The difficulty selection algorithm diagram communicates branch logic effectively. Consider summarizing key decision criteria in accompanying text.
While the description notes three difficulty levels, the actual differences (e.g., time allocation, riddle pool size) arenât specified. Define level parameters.
